description: Invariant rules and constraints for NA - Recolor node (color + pattern-aware)
invariants: Do not change the silhouette or proportions of the garment
Do not alter materials, textures, or surface qualities (yarn weight, knit gauge, weave pattern, fiber sheen)
Do not modify seams, stitching, buttons, zippers, or any construction details
Do not change the lighting setup, direction, intensity, or quality
Do not alter the white balance or introduce any color cast
Do not apply any color grading, filters, or artistic effects
Do not add cinematic looks, vintage tones, or stylized processing
Maintain uniform color saturation across the entire garment
Preserve shadow positions, depths, and gradients exactly as in the original
Preserve highlight positions, intensities, and falloff exactly as in the original
The result must appear as an authentic studio product photograph
Color must be readable and accurate under neutral studio lighting
invariants_formatted:
STRICT CONSTRAINTS:
- Preserve silhouette and proportions exactly
- Maintain all materials, textures, and surface qualities unchanged
- Keep all seams, stitching, and construction details intact
- Do not change lighting setup or white balance
- No color grading, no filters, no artistic effects
- No cinematic or stylized processing
- Ensure uniform color saturation across garment
- Preserve original shadow and highlight positions
- Result must appear as authentic studio photograph

pattern_handling.description: Rules for applying reference images that contain repeating patterns or fabric motifs
rules: If the reference image contains a repeating pattern, treat it as a fabric pattern source, not a flat color source
Preserve pattern scale and density relative to the garment size
Align pattern direction and repetition consistently across the garment panels and sleeves
Do not stretch, warp, or drift the pattern across seams and edges
The pattern must appear integrated into the fabric (knitted or woven-in) rather than a flat printed overlay
Do not flatten or erase knit relief when applying a pattern; cables, ribs, and yarn definition must remain visible
Maintain neutral studio lighting and preserve shadows and highlights exactly as in the original
pattern_handling.pattern_invariants_formatted:
PATTERN CONSTRAINTS:
- Treat reference as fabric pattern source when repeating motifs exist
- Preserve pattern scale, density, and repetition rhythm
- Keep alignment consistent across front and sleeves
- No stretching, warping, drifting, or misregistration
- Pattern must look knitted or woven-in, not printed on top
- Preserve knit relief and microtexture
- Maintain original lighting, shadows, and highlights

color_source_priority.description: Priority order when multiple color sources are provided
color_source_priority.conflict_resolution: If color_reference_image is present and valid, it overrides pantone dropdown selection

strict.description: Maximum fidelity to original; only color or pattern changes

balanced.description: Slight adjustments allowed for color harmony (not recommended for product color matching)

error_messages.no_color_source: No color source provided. Please select a Pantone color or provide a color reference image.
error_messages.invalid_pantone: Pantone code '{code}' not found in palette. Please select a valid Pantone from the dropdown.
error_messages.invalid_reference_image: Color reference image could not be processed. Falling back to Pantone selection if available.
error_messages.no_garment_image: Garment image is required. Please connect a garment image to the node.
